task_completion.description: The TitanicSurvival dataset is loaded into Jamovi.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Jamovi window is open displaying the TitanicSurvival dataset with columns like 'survived', 'sex', and 'passengerClass'.

task_completion.description: Log-Linear Regression analysis is selected with the correct nominal factors.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Log-Linear Regression analysis panel is open. 'survived', 'sex', and 'passengerClass' are placed in the 'Factors' box and are treated as nominal variables (indicated by the Venn diagram-like nominal icon next to them).

task_completion.description: The model is configured with main effects and two-way interactions, excluding the three-way interaction.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 'Model' section of the analysis options, the Model Terms list includes the main effects and all two-way interactions, but the three-way interaction (survived*sex*passengerClass) is absent.

task_completion.description: The Jamovi project is saved to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog or the Jamovi window title indicates the project is saved as 'TitanicLogLinear.omv' in the /home/ga/Documents/Jamovi/ directory.

task_completion.description: The text report contains the correct Deviance and Degrees of Freedom.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor is open showing 'loglinear_report.txt' with the Deviance value of 47.17 and Degrees of Freedom (DF) of 2.

task_completion.description: The text report contains the correct p-value and conclusion.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text editor showing 'loglinear_report.txt' includes a p-value of <0.0001 (or 0.0000) and the word 'INADEQUATE'.

integrity.description: The agent used the Jamovi GUI to perform the analysis.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Jamovi graphical interface, not just terminal commands or Python scripts executing the analysis.

integrity.description: The results in the text report match the output generated in Jamovi.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Jamovi results panel displays the Deviance (47.17), df (2), and p-value (<.0001), which correspond to the values typed into the text report.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply type the expected answers without running the analysis.
integrity.visual_evidence: There is visual evidence of the Log-Linear Regression actually being computed in Jamovi before the text report is created.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The correct Log-Linear Regression model on the TitanicSurvival dataset (main effects + two-way interactions for 'survived', 'sex', and 'passengerClass') yields a Deviance of 47.17, 2 Degrees of Freedom, and a p-value of <0.0001. Because the p-value is <= 0.05, the conclusion must be 'INADEQUATE'.
